stimulus: In her 1997 book The Cultivated Plate: Food and Identity at the Modern Table, anthropologist Diana Yusof first developed at length her notion of a “gastronomic memory”—the proposition that the assorted communal and ritual scripts an eater has absorbed (region, religion, generation, etc.) ______blank every flavor judgment that eater makes at a meal.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The plural verb "condition" agrees in number with the plural subject "scripts."

Choice B is incorrect because the singular verb "conditions" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "scripts." Choice C is incorrect because the singular verb "is conditioning" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "scripts." Choice D is incorrect because the singular verb "has been conditioning" doesn't agree in number with the plural subject "scripts."
